description_long: This is a medium-sized donkey with a robust, rounded body covered in short, grayish-brown fur. Its head is large and expressive, featuring a prominent, rounded snout and long, upright ears with darker tips and inner edges. The donkey's legs are sturdy and end in small, dark hooves, while its tail is thin and ends in a distinct tuft of black hair. The mane is short, upright, and bristly, running along the top of the neck and contrasting in color with the rest of the body. Subtle shading and textural details are visible throughout, giving the animal a lifelike and slightly stylized appearance suitable for urban or park environments.
description: A grayish-brown donkey with long upright ears, a bristly mane, and a tufted tail, featuring sturdy legs and a rounded body.
description_view_0: Front view showing the donkey's large, rounded snout, wide-set eyes, and tall, upright ears.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the donkey's profile, bristly mane, and the full length of its tail with a tuft at the end.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the donkey's back, tail with tuft, and the symmetry of its hind legs and hooves.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the donkey's facial profile, mane, and tail, with the mouth slightly open.
attribute_ears: The donkey's ears are very long, upright, and narrow, positioned symmetrically on top of the head. They have darker tips and inner edges, with a slightly lighter outer surface and a subtle texture that suggests short fur.
attribute_tail: The tail is thin and of medium length, extending straight from the base of the spine and ending in a prominent, bushy black tuft. The tail itself is lighter in color, matching the body, with the tuft providing a strong visual contrast.
attribute_hooves: There are four small, rounded hooves, each dark in color and smooth in texture. The hooves are compact and proportionate to the donkey's legs, with no visible markings.
attribute_mane: The mane is short, upright, and bristly, running along the top of the neck. It is black in color, contrasting with the lighter gray-brown body, and appears coarse in texture.
